task_description: Uninstall a specific installed application from the system settings.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a gray gear icon labeled “设置” with a semitransparent red X centered directly on it, indicating the Windows Settings app tile. In the full screen, the Windows Start menu is open in the center-left with pinned apps and recommended items, and a phone companion panel is open on the right. The target is a UI control inside the Start menu app grid.
caption.action: Click the Settings app tile with the gray gear icon labeled “设置”.
caption.expectation: The Settings window should open, replacing or appearing above the Start menu.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the left sidebar item labeled “应用”, which is a navigation control in the Settings window. In the full screen, Windows Settings is open in Chinese with the page header “应用 > 安装的应用”, and the main panel lists installed apps such as Apple Mobile Device Support, BlueStacks, and Bonjour. The target is a sidebar UI control that is already highlighted.
caption.action: Click the “应用” item in the left sidebar.
caption.expectation: The Apps section remains selected or refreshes, keeping the installed apps page visible in the main panel.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on an empty light-gray strip between list sections, with only partial text from the row above visible at the left edge; there is no button, icon, checkbox, or handle directly under the X, so the target is a blank region in the content area. In the full screen, the Windows Settings app is open in Chinese on the “应用” page, showing a vertical list of app-related options such as installed apps, advanced app settings, default apps, and others, with the left sidebar still highlighting “应用”.
caption.action: Click the blank light-gray area in the main Apps content panel.
caption.expectation: Nothing significant should open; focus may remain on the Settings window and any prior selection state should stay unchanged.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the full Settings window rather than a tightly targeted control, with no semitransparent red X. The visible page is Windows Settings in Chinese at “应用 > 启动”, showing a scrollable list of startup apps such as Intel Graphics Command Center Startup Task, Lenovo Hotkeys, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Microsoft Defender, Microsoft Edge, Microsoft OneDrive, Power Automate Desktop, Realtek HD Audio Universal Service, and Steam++ System Boot Run, each with a toggle on the right. In the full screen, the left sidebar still has “应用” highlighted, the search bar is at the top, and the main startup-apps panel is the active scrollable region.
caption.action: Scroll down in the startup apps list within the main Settings content panel.
caption.expectation: The startup applications list should move downward to reveal more items further below on the page.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the left navigation item labeled “应用” in the Windows Settings sidebar. It is a sidebar UI control, shown as a light-gray highlighted row among other categories like 网络和 Internet and 个性化. In the full screen, the Settings window is already on “应用 > 启动”, with the main panel listing startup apps such as Intel Graphics Command Center Startup Task, Lenovo Hotkeys, Microsoft 365 Copilot, Microsoft Defender, Microsoft Edge, Microsoft OneDrive, Power Automate Desktop, Realtek HD Audio Universal Service, Steam++ System Boot Run, VMware Tray Process, and Windows Security notification icon, each with status text and a toggle on the right.
caption.action: Click the “应用” item in the left sidebar.
caption.expectation: The Apps section should remain selected or refresh, while the Settings window stays focused and the startup apps page remains visible in the main panel.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the top list item labeled “安装的应用,” with smaller gray subtitle text below indicating it is for uninstalling and managing apps on this PC. This is a rectangular row-style UI control in the main Settings content area, not a blank region. In the full screen, the Windows Settings app is open to the “应用” section in Chinese, with multiple app-related options listed such as advanced app settings, default apps, optional features, video playback, and startup; the left sidebar has “应用” highlighted.
caption.action: Click the “安装的应用” row in the main Apps panel.
caption.expectation: The Installed apps page should open, showing the list of installed applications and related management options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows the full Windows Settings window with no semitransparent red X, indicating a scroll action rather than a targeted control. The current page is “应用 > 安装的应用,” and the main content area contains a vertically scrollable list of installed programs. Visible entries include “开始体验”应用, 《使命召唤：现代战争 II 2022》, 360压缩, Anaconda3 2024.10-1 (Python 3.12.7 64-bit), Android Studio, Apple Mobile Device Support, Apple Software Update, Application Insights Tools for Visual Studio 2015, Bitz Net 1.5.4, BlueStacks, and Bonjour, each in separate white rows with app icons, publisher/version/date details, and three-dot menus on the right. In the full screen, the left sidebar still has “应用” highlighted, the breadcrumb title at the top reads “应用 > 安装的应用,” and the right-side scrollbar indicates the app list panel is the active scrollable region.
caption.action: Scroll down in the installed apps list within the main Settings content area.
caption.expectation: The installed applications list should move downward, revealing more apps further below the currently visible entries.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows a semitransparent red X centered on the horizontal three-dots button at the right end of the “Apple Software Update” app row, next to the app size text “4.05 MB.” This is a row action menu control, not a blank area. In the full screen, Windows Settings is open to “应用 > 安装的应用,” showing the installed apps list with entries such as Android Studio, Apple Mobile Device Support, Apple Software Update, Application Insights Tools for Visual Studio 2015, Bitz Net 1.5.4, BlueStacks, and Bonjour, each with a three-dots menu on the far right.
caption.action: Click the three-dots menu button on the “Apple Software Update” row.
caption.expectation: A small context menu for Apple Software Update should open, showing available actions for that installed app.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The crop shows a small rounded white context menu opened from the Apple Software Update row, with two stacked options: “修改” on top and “卸载” below. The semitransparent red X is centered on the lower “卸载” menu item, indicating a click on that menu control rather than a blank area. In the full screen, Windows Settings is open to “应用 > 安装的应用,” the installed apps list is visible, and the Apple Software Update row’s three-dots menu is expanded with this two-item menu.
caption.action: Click the “卸载” option in the Apple Software Update context menu.
caption.expectation: The context menu should close and Windows should proceed to the uninstall flow for Apple Software Update, likely showing a confirmation prompt or launching the app's uninstaller.
